title: Lepidaploa sagrana
taxonomy: Lepidaploa sagrana is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and morphological features. Lepidaploa sagrana is also classified under the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification places it within the order and the class due to its shared characteristics with other plants in these groups. Additionally, it is classified as a species within the genus Lepidaploa.
uses: Lepidaploa sagrana is a plant species used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its medicinal uses are still being researched and documented, but it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
